summary: Mixed performance with uneven vocals and a rough mix, but flashes of brilliance like a ripping Little Red Rooster keep it interesting.
review: The July 1995 show is uneven – the recording sounds off‑balance and Jerry Garcia’s vocals suffer from his ill health, leading some listeners to recommend skipping it.  Still, a few moments such as a spirited Little Red Rooster (and a well‑executed “Masterpiece” segment) provide enough merit for dedicated Deadheads who want to hear every era.

ai_rating.rationale: Only two substantive reviews (4★ and 2★) with clear opinions; one praises the show while the other condemns vocal and mix quality, yielding an overall low‑mid rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Reviewers note a “off” SMR mix and badly flubbed vocals; no specific mention of soundboard or audience source.

show_quality.setlist_flow: No detailed commentary on set pacing; the show is described as generally inconsistent with a few high points.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia was sick during this performance, resulting in noticeably weak vocals.
